skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Diagnose context degradation in agent systems and choose mitigation patterns for long conversations, large context windows, and degraded responses.
skill.summary: Diagnose and reduce context degradation in long-running AI agent systems.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these were false positives from Markdown code fences and terms such as degradation, critical, and warning. No evidence found of command execution, networking, credential access, prompt injection, malicious intent, or cryptographic routines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick fences as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed locations are documentation examples, not executable shell invocations, and no command execution API was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fenced examples and explanatory text. Review found no subprocess, shell, eval, or exec usage in these files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Terminology
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated words related to degradation and critical states as weak cryptography indicators. The reviewed files discuss AI context behavior and health scores, not encryption, hashing, cipher selection, or credential protection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched terms are domain language for AI context degradation. No cryptographic library use, hash function, cipher, key handling, or authentication logic was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged references to system prompts, tool definitions, or production systems. These are conceptual references about preserving context elements and do not inspect host configuration or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain explanatory language and static string lists. No OS fingerprinting, environment enumeration, network probing, or host metadata collection was found.

content.value_statement: Long AI agent sessions can lose accuracy when context becomes crowded or conflicting. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code identify degradation patterns and select practical mitigations.
actual_capabilities: Explains lost-in-the-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash patterns.
Provides guidance for placing critical information at attention-favored positions.
Describes mitigation strategies such as compaction, masking, partitioning, and isolation.
Includes reference examples for monitoring context health and alert thresholds.
Provides a Python utility for estimating context health and degradation symptoms.
limitations: The Python detector uses heuristic simulations, not actual model attention weights.
Benchmark numbers in the documentation may require independent verification before production use.
The skill does not automatically repair degraded conversations.
The reference examples contain pseudocode functions that require implementation.

use_cases.title: Debug Long Agent Sessions
use_cases.description: Find why an agent starts ignoring goals, choosing wrong tools, or repeating earlier mistakes.

use_cases.title: Design Context Windows
use_cases.description: Plan where to place goals, constraints, retrieved documents, and summaries in long prompts.

use_cases.title: Review Reliability Failures
use_cases.description: Classify failures as poisoning, distraction, confusion, clash, or lost-in-the-middle behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a long prompt or conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent context for degradation risks. Identify likely patterns and give three practical changes I can make now.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging after an agent produced a poor result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this failure transcript. Decide whether the main cause is lost-in-the-middle, poisoning, distraction, confusion, or clash. Explain the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Mitigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced redesign of a context strategy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context mitigation plan for this workflow. Include placement rules, compaction triggers, retrieval filtering, and isolation boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evaluation plan for detecting context degradation in production agent sessions. Include metrics, thresholds, test cases, and review cadence.

output_examples.input: An agent ignored a requirement that appeared in the middle of a long task brief.
output_examples.output: Likely lost-in-the-middle behavior. Move the requirement to the opening task block and repeat it in the final acceptance criteria.

output_examples.input: A support agent keeps using outdated policy text after being corrected.
output_examples.output: Likely context poisoning or clash. Remove the outdated policy from active context and mark the corrected source as authoritative.

output_examples.input: A coding agent selected tools for an earlier task after the user changed goals.
output_examples.output: Likely context confusion. Start a new task segment, restate active tools, and archive unrelated earlier instructions.

best_practices: Place goals, constraints, and acceptance criteria at the start or end of context.
Filter retrieved documents before adding them to active context.
Use compaction before context size causes observable quality loss.
anti_patterns: Adding every available document to context without relevance filtering.
Keeping corrected hallucinations in the active conversation without removal.
Mixing unrelated tasks in one long agent session without clear boundaries.

faq.question: What problem does this skill address?
faq.answer: It helps identify why AI agents perform worse as context grows or becomes conflicting.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No evidence of external command execution was found in the reviewed skill files.

faq.question: Can it measure real model attention?
faq.answer: No. The included detector simulates attention patterns and should be replaced with model-specific instrumentation for production.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for very large contexts?
faq.answer: No. It can also help with smaller contexts that contain irrelevant, outdated, or conflicting information.

faq.question: How should teams apply the recommendations?
faq.answer: Start with context placement, relevance filtering, and compaction triggers, then add isolation for complex workflows.
